---
format:
  gfm:
    default-image-extension: ""
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.qmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r}
#| include: false
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# tabular

<!-- badges: start -->
[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/vthanik/tabular/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/vthanik/tabular/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![Lifecycle: experimental](https://img.shields.io/badge/lifecycle-experimental-orange.svg)](https://lifecycle.r-lib.org/articles/stages.html#experimental)
<!-- badges: end -->

Native emission of clinical submission tables to RTF, LaTeX, HTML,
PDF, and DOCX. Decimal alignment via real font metrics, multi-level
column headers with passthrough leaves, predicate-targeted styling,
and group-aware pagination — all from one short pipeline. Designed
for CDISC ADaM workflows and FDA / EMA / PMDA regulatory submissions.

## Installation

You can install the development version of tabular from GitHub:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("vthanik/tabular")
```

## Status

`tabular` is in **pre-CRAN** development. The spec-building and
resolve-engine surface (`tabular()`, `cols()`, `headers()`,
`sort_rows()`, `derive()`, `style()`) is in place; backends
(`emit()` to RTF / LaTeX / PDF / HTML / DOCX) land incrementally per
`plan.md`. Break-anything-anytime until v0.1.0.

## Example

The pipeline starts from a pre-summarised wide data frame and chains
through one verb per concern. Each verb returns an updated
`tabular_spec`; the engine resolves the spec at render time:

``` r
library(tabular)

n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short)

tabular(
  saf_demo,
  titles = c(
    "Table 14.1.1",
    "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",
    sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"])
  ),
  footnotes = "Percentages based on N per treatment group."
) |>
  cols(
    variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"),
    stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),
    placebo    = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo\nN=%d",  n["placebo"]),  align = "decimal"),
    drug_50    = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50\nN=%d",  n["drug_50"]),  align = "decimal"),
    drug_100   = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100\nN=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal"),
    Total      = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total\nN=%d",    n["Total"]),    align = "decimal")
  ) |>
  headers(
    "Treatment Group" = c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total")
  ) |>
  sort_rows(by = c("variable", "stat_label"))
```

When the render backends land, the final verb in the chain will
emit to a file:

``` r
# Pending implementation:
... |> emit("t_14_1_1.rtf")
```

## Verb surface (as of this snapshot)

| Verb | Tier | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `tabular()` | Entry | Wrap a pre-summarised data frame into a `tabular_spec` |
| `pivot_across()` | Input helper | Bridge `cards` long ARD to a wide display data frame |
| `col_spec()` | Build | Per-column display attributes (usage / label / align / format) |
| `cols()` | Build | Attach `col_spec` entries to the spec, keyed by data column |
| `headers()` | Build | Multi-level column-header bands; passthrough leaves supported |
| `sort_rows()` | Build | Output row order; factor-level-aware, NA-last |
| `derive()` | Build | Display-time row-arithmetic; topo-sorted; references prior derives |
| `style()` | Style | Predicate-targeted bold / colour / borders for cells and rows |

## Demo datasets

Pre-summarised wide-format tables and their upstream `cards`-format
counterparts ship with the package to power examples, vignettes, and
tests:

| Dataset | Content |
|---|---|
| `saf_demo` / `saf_demo_card` | Demographics, Safety Population |
| `saf_aeoverall` / `saf_aeoverall_card` | High-level AE flags |
| `saf_aesocpt` / `saf_aesocpt_card` | AEs by SOC and Preferred Term |
| `saf_vital` / `saf_vital_card` | Vital signs at Baseline / End of Treatment |
| `eff_resp` / `eff_resp_card` | Best Overall Response and Response Rates |
| `saf_n` / `eff_n` | BigN denominators per arm |
